text: The surgeon demonstrates a robotic ventral hernia repair using a preperitoneal tap technique. A visible dimple indicates the hernia location. The surgeon establishes the correct plane for the peritoneal flap, entering the preperitoneal space while avoiding the retro-rectus space. Inferiorly, the space is developed, revealing some fat and potentially an arcuate line, ensuring the mesh remains outside the abdominal cavity.

The surgeon works superiorly, maintaining the preperitoneal plane while leaving the posterior layer of the rectus sheath intact. Sharp and electrocautery dissection is used to develop the space, revealing the hernia defect with a volcano sign as the hernia protrudes into the preperitoneal space. The surgeon removes preperitoneal fat from the defect, ensuring the peritoneal flap remains intact and the layers are preserved.

The surgeon uses a locking V-lock suture to close the main hernia defect, taking bites of fascia above the defect and imbricating the hernia sac to reduce seroma formation. The suture is left loose initially and tightened sequentially. The surgeon then addresses a smaller port site hernia defect inferiorly, using the same stitch technique to close it with healthy fascial bites on either side.

The surgeon completes the suturing by locking the V-lock suture back on itself and cutting it to close the defect. A piece of permanent synthetic mesh is placed in the pre-peritoneal space with wide overlap. The mesh is fixated using Vicryl interrupted sutures at the four corners to ensure it remains flat and tented, preventing wrinkling or curling. The mesh is further fixated inferiorly using a single suture for efficiency. Finally, the peritoneal flap is closed with another V-lock suture and absorbable trio.

The surgeon demonstrates the pre-peritoneal tap technique, ensuring the mesh is positioned outside the abdominal cavity, avoiding exposure to the bowel. The procedure utilizes three 8mm ports for minimal incisions, achieving a wide mesh overlap. The preperitoneal space is closed with the mesh securely excluded from the abdominal cavity, completing the repair.
